Search results for "llvmlite PassManager optimization pipeline example":

Title: LLVM's New Pass Manager : r/Compilers - Reddit
URL: https://www.reddit.com/r/Compilers/comments/mfkvki/llvms_new_pass_manager
Content: # LLVM’s New Pass Manager : r/Compilers. Skip to main contentLLVM’s New Pass Manager : r/Compilers. # LLVM’s New Pass Manager. I've used the new pass manager in my compiler to create a simple series of optimizations (each pass is run once, nothing fancy), and it seems to work well. I like the nested nature of the passes, where loop pass managers can be added as passes to function pass managers and function pass managers to module pass managers in a pretty intuitive way. Most passes have been updated to work with the new pass manager, but you can be sure this is the case if the pass inherits from an instantiation of `PassInfoMixin<>` and has a member function that looks something like:. Once all of your passes are added, then you create a `PassBuilder` object, use it to register each analysis manager, and cross-register the analysis managers (this is pretty simple, see example).

Title: Optimization passes - llvmlite - Read the Docs
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/v0.45.0rc1/user-guide/binding/optimization-passes.html
Content: # Optimization passes¶. Optimization passes are managed by a pass manager. llvmlite provides bindings for LLVM’s *New* and *Legacy* pass managers, which have slightly different APIs and behaviour. The differences between them and the motivations for the New Pass Manager are outlined in the LLVM Blog post on the New Pass Manager. It is recommended that new code using llvmlite uses the New Pass Manager, and existing code using the Legacy Pass Manager be updated to use the New Pass Manager. ## New Pass Manager APIs¶. :   The level of optimization for size, as an integer between 0 and 2. :   A pass manager for running optimization passes on an LLVM module. :   A pass manager for running optimization passes on an LLVM function. :   Create a new pass manager to run optimization passes on a module. :   Create a new pass manager to run optimization passes on a function of the given *module*, a `ModuleRef` instance.

Title: Optimization passes - llvmlite - Read the Docs
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/binding/optimization-passes.html
Content: # Optimization passes¶. Optimization passes are managed by a pass manager. llvmlite provides bindings for LLVM’s *New Pass Manager*. Prior to llvmlite 0.45 (LLVM 20), llvmlite also supported the *Legacy Pass Manager*, which had a different API and behavior. The differences between them and the motivations for the New Pass Manager are outlined in the LLVM Blog post on the New Pass Manager. As of llvmlite 0.45 (LLVM 20), support for the Legacy Pass Manager has been removed. ## New Pass Manager APIs¶. :   The level of optimization for size, as an integer between 0 and 2. :   A pass manager for running optimization passes on an LLVM module. :   A pass manager for running optimization passes on an LLVM function. :   Create a new pass manager to run optimization passes on a module. :   Create a new pass manager to run optimization passes on a function of the given *module*, a `ModuleRef` instance.

Title: Making the LLVM Legacy Pass Manager 1.65x Faster :: Neil Henning
URL: https://www.neilhenning.dev/posts/making-legacy-pass-manager-1_65x-faster
Content: One of the things I was going to talk about in my COVID cancelled EuroLLVM 2020 talk was about a neat little trick we used to get the LLVM legacy pass manager to be significantly faster. LLVM has a default pass optimization pipeline that you create with the `PassManagerBuilder` class:. llvm::legacy::PassManager passManager; llvm::legacy::PassManager passManager; ::::   llvm::PassManagerBuilder passManagerBuilder; llvm::PassManagerBuilder passManagerBuilder; ::   // We can set the optimization level (-O0 ..

Title: [llvm-dev] [RFC] Changing the default pass manager for ...
URL: https://groups.google.com/g/llvm-dev/c/Im829naY0PI/m/M9_zHyFJAQAJ
Content: # [llvm-dev] [RFC] Changing the default pass manager for the optimization pipeline. We've been fixing the various remaining issues in order to turn on the new pass manager for the optimization pipeline, and it's about time to turn it on. There's also the LLVM C API and bugpoint that still use the legacy PM, which can be ported later on and only block the removal of the legacy PM. >We've been fixing the various remaining issues in order to turn on the new. >pass manager for the optimization pipeline, and it's about time to turn it. >codegen pipeline since that's mostly not been ported to use the new PM. >There's also the LLVM C API and bugpoint that still use the legacy PM,. >which can be ported later on and only block the removal of the legacy PM.
